title: Failed Authentication Burst Detection on Payment Gateway Accounts

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 10.2.4 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 10.2.4 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.11.49: Failed Authentication Burst Detection on Payment Gateway Accounts.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.49' running on index=windows EventCode 4625; index=linux linux:secure failed password, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
spl:
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4625 earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=linux sourcetype="linux:secure" "failed password" earliest=-24h)
| eval acct=coalesce(TargetUserName,user)
| lookup pci_payment_accounts.csv account AS acct OUTPUT gateway_acct
| where gateway_acct="true"
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count by _time, acct, src_ip
| where count>25
| sort - count
description: Trends failed logon attempts against payment application service accounts and operator IDs so brute-force activity is visible and aligned with monitoring of invalid access attempts.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes linux, windows and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security, linux:secure. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Populate payment service account list. (2) Whitelist vulnerability scanner IPs. (3) Auto-block at firewall for sustained bursts. (4) Daily SOC review metrics. Indexes required: `index=linux`, `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `sourcetype=linux:secure`; Lookups: `pci_payment_accounts`, `pci_payment_accounts.csv`.
visualization: Timechart (failed logons), table (acct, src_ip, count), heatmap (acct x hour).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t dc(Vulnerabilities.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - agg_value

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux

knownFalsePositives: Scan failures during legacy system lifecycle, documented compensating controls, or planned upgrade windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=windows` EventCode 4625; `index=linux` `linux:secure` `failed password`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Populate payment service account list. (2) Whitelist vulnerability scanner IPs. (3) Auto-block at firewall for sustained bursts. (4) Daily SOC review metrics.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4625 earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=linux sourcetype="linux:secure" "failed password" earliest=-24h)
| eval acct=coalesce(TargetUserName,user)
| lookup pci_payment_accounts.csv account AS acct OUTPUT gateway_acct
| where gateway_acct="true"
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count by _time, acct, src_ip
| where count>25
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Failed Authentication Burst Detection on Payment Gateway Accounts (PCI DSS Req 8.3.4, 10.2.4)** ‚Äî Trends failed logon attempts against payment application service accounts and operator IDs so brute-force activity is visible and aligned with monitoring of invalid access attempts.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` EventCode 4625; `index=linux` `linux:secure` `failed password`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows, linux; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security, linux:secure. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=windows, index=linux, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **acct** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where gateway_acct="true"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, acct, src_ip** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where count>25` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.src span=5m | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Failed Authentication Burst Detection on Payment Gateway Accounts (PCI DSS Req 8.3.4, 10.2.4)** ‚Äî Trends failed logon attempts against payment application service accounts and operator IDs so brute-force activity is visible and aligned with monitoring of invalid access attempts.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` EventCode 4625; `index=linux` `linux:secure` `failed password`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timechart (failed logons), table (acct, src_ip, count), heatmap (acct x hour).
grandmaExplanation: We trends failed logon attempts against payment application service accounts and operator IDs so brute-force activity is visible and aligned with monitoring of invalid access attempts. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Vulnerabilities (CIM data model).
